raw_output_payload.reasoning: We need a bash command that pings a hostname, greps for 192.168.11, and prints the IP. Probably use ping -c 1 hostname | grep -oP '(?<=\().*?(?=\))' but filter 192.168.11. So: ping -c 1 hostname | grep -oP '\b192\.168\.11\.\d+\b' . That prints IP. So final command: ping -c 1 hostname | grep -oP '\b192\.168\.11\.\d+\b'.

Probably use ping -c 1 hostname | grep -oP '(?<=\().*?(?=\))' but filter 192.168.11. So: ping -c 1 hostname | grep -oP '\b192\.168\.11\.\d+\b' . That prints IP. So final command: ping -c 1 hostname | grep -oP '\b192\.168\.11\.\d+\b'.

task.query: Ping hostname, grep for 192.168.11 and print the IP from the output
task.gold: ping -c 1 hostname | grep 192.168.11 | grep 'bytes from' | awk '{print $4}' | sed 's/://g'
